description_long: This apartment building is a tall, rectangular high-rise structure with a beige exterior frame and a grid of uniformly spaced balconies and windows covering its facade. The building features a repetitive, modular design with vertical white columns separating the rows of balconies, giving it a sense of symmetry and order. Each floor appears to have a continuous balcony running the length of the building, with metal railings and glass panels. The roof is flat and unadorned, matching the beige color of the building's frame. The overall appearance is utilitarian and functional, with minimal decorative elements. The structure is typical of dense urban environments, designed to maximize residential capacity.
description: A tall, beige high-rise apartment building with a grid of balconies and windows, separated by vertical white columns and a flat roof. The design is symmetrical and utilitarian, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full grid of balconies and windows, with vertical white columns and a flat beige roof.
description_view_1: This view is nearly identical to the front, showing the same grid of balconies and windows, with slight perspective changes.
description_view_2: The third view continues the symmetrical pattern, with the same arrangement of balconies and windows visible.
description_view_3: The fourth view mirrors the previous ones, maintaining the consistent grid and vertical column design.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, grid-like pattern, with each window aligned vertically and horizontally, separated by white columns and balcony railings.
